channel.title: Light Tower Group — NYC CRE Capital Markets Insights
channel.description: Data-driven capital markets analysis and building intelligence for NYC commercial real estate professionals.

item.title: Blackstone's $618B AUM Tops IRE Ranking as Top 50 Managers Hit $4.8T
item.description: Top 50 real estate managers hit $4.8T AUM, up from $4.4T. Blackstone leads with $618B. Interest rates top risk for 85% of managers. Geopolitical risk surges.

news:news.news:title: Blackstone's $618B AUM Tops IRE Ranking as Top 50 Managers Hit $4.8T
news:news.news:keywords: Blackstone, AUM, real estate investment managers, interest rates, geopolitical risk

item.title: Treasuries Near Three-Week High as Jobs Data May Cement Warsh Rate Hike Case
item.description: Treasuries hover near three-week high ahead of June 2026 employment data that could cement case for Fed Chair Kevin Warsh to deliver an interest-rate hike. Bond market signals tightening.

news:news.news:title: Treasuries Near Three-Week High as Jobs Data May Cement Warsh Rate Hike Case
news:news.news:keywords: Treasuries, Federal Reserve, Kevin Warsh, interest rates, employment data

item.title: Walker & Dunlop Captures Top Spot in $16.5B Fannie Mae Market as Refinancing Dominates
item.description: Walker & Dunlop leads Fannie Mae multifamily lending with $2.18B in 2026 YTD originations. Top 10 lenders capture 78% of $16.5B market as refinancing accounts for 62.8% of volume.

news:news.news:title: Walker & Dunlop Captures Top Spot in $16.5B Fannie Mae Market as Refinancing Dominates
news:news.news:keywords: Walker & Dunlop, Fannie Mae, multifamily lending, agency debt, CRED iQ

item.title: Simpson Thacher Doubles Down on Midtown with 916K-SF Lease at Extell's 570 Fifth
item.description: Simpson Thacher & Bartlett signs 916K-SF lease at Extell's 570 Fifth Avenue, 200K SF larger than original deal. The law firm will occupy 26 of 29 stories in the 2028-delivery tower, signaling institutional tenant demand for new-build office space.

news:news.news:title: Simpson Thacher Doubles Down on Midtown with 916K-SF Lease at Extell's 570 Fifth
news:news.news:keywords: Simpson Thacher, Extell Development, 570 Fifth Avenue, Manhattan office lease, law firm relocation

item.title: Manhattan Office Leasing Hits 19.6M SF Year-to-Date, Best Pace Since 2000
item.description: Manhattan office leasing hits 19.6M SF YTD, best since 2000. May volume up 35% YoY. Availability drops to 13.2%, rents rise to $77.76. Simpson Thacher, Google, Versant drive activity.

news:news.news:title: Manhattan Office Leasing Hits 19.6M SF Year-to-Date, Best Pace Since 2000
news:news.news:keywords: Manhattan office, leasing volume, Colliers, Simpson Thacher, availability rate, asking rent, Midtown

item.title: BHI Bets $170M on Third Avenue Office-to-Resi Conversion as Lenders Pick Winners
item.description: BHI provides $169.5M construction loan for Quantum Pacific and MetroLoft's 767 Third Avenue office-to-residential conversion, adding to $143M in prior bridge financing.

news:news.news:title: BHI Bets $170M on Third Avenue Office-to-Resi Conversion as Lenders Pick Winners
news:news.news:keywords: BHI, MetroLoft, Quantum Pacific Realty, office-to-residential conversion, construction financing

item.title: Berkshire's $6.8B Taylor Morrison Bet: Abel's First Big Move Signals Housing Cycle Turn
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway acquires Taylor Morrison for $6.8B in cash, a 24% premium. Greg Abel's first major deal signals a contrarian bet on a housing recovery as mortgage rates remain elevated.

news:news.news:title: Berkshire's $6.8B Taylor Morrison Bet: Abel's First Big Move Signals Housing Cycle Turn
news:news.news:keywords: Berkshire Hathaway, Taylor Morrison, Greg Abel, homebuilding, M&A, housing market, Warren Buffett

item.title: SL Green Sheds 10 East 53rd Street at $312M as Office REIT Executes $2.5B Disposition Plan
item.description: SL Green sells 10 East 53rd Street to Meadow Partners for $312M, netting $100M for debt paydown. The 92%-leased Midtown tower highlights institutional demand for well-located office assets.

news:news.news:title: SL Green Sheds 10 East 53rd Street at $312M as Office REIT Executes $2.5B Disposition Plan
news:news.news:keywords: SL Green, Meadow Partners, 10 East 53rd Street, office sales, New York City

item.title: China Vanke Cedes Bush Tower to Lender at 52% Discount
item.description: China Vanke transfers 130 West 42nd Street to United Overseas Bank for $58.1M, a 52% discount to the 2018 loan. The Midtown office tower's distress signals deepening CRE credit losses and Chinese capital flight.

news:news.news:title: China Vanke Cedes Bush Tower to Lender at 52% Discount
news:news.news:keywords: China Vanke, Bush Tower, United Overseas Bank, Midtown office, distressed CRE, lender takeover, Chinese capital

item.title: Rockrose Locks $404M Freddie Mac Loan at 666 Greenwich as Agency Liquidity Floods Multifamily
item.description: Rockrose Development secures $404M Freddie Mac loan from PNC Bank for 479-unit Archive at 666 Greenwich. Agency liquidity drives luxury multifamily refinancing as rate cycle shifts.

news:news.news:title: Rockrose Locks $404M Freddie Mac Loan at 666 Greenwich as Agency Liquidity Floods Multifamily
news:news.news:keywords: Rockrose Development, Freddie Mac, PNC Bank, 666 Greenwich, multifamily refinance

item.title: Atalian Bondholders Seize Control in Debt Restructuring
item.description: Atalian SAS bondholders agree to take control of the French facilities management firm in a debt restructuring, signaling creditor discipline in European CRE.

news:news.news:title: Atalian Bondholders Seize Control in Debt Restructuring
news:news.news:keywords: Atalian, debt restructuring, bondholders, European CRE, creditor control

item.title: Mamdani's $5B Housing Plan: 200,000 Units or a Fiscal Gamble?
item.description: Mayor Mamdani's Block by Block plan targets 200,000 affordable homes with $5B in funding, transit-oriented zoning, and a revolving loan fund. Can the city deliver?

news:news.news:title: Mamdani's $5B Housing Plan: 200,000 Units or a Fiscal Gamble?
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, New York City, Zohran Mamdani, land-use reform, transit-oriented development

item.title: SMBC Nikko Eyes $627M Japan Mezzanine Fund as M&A Boom Strains Senior Lending
item.description: SMBC Nikko Securities mulls a $627 million Japan mezzanine fund to capture demand from a booming M&A market where senior lenders are maxed out. The move signals a structural shift in Japanese capital stack dynamics.

news:news.news:title: SMBC Nikko Eyes $627M Japan Mezzanine Fund as M&A Boom Strains Senior Lending
news:news.news:keywords: SMBC Nikko, Japan mezzanine fund, M&A boom, mezzanine financing, Japan capital markets

item.title: PERE 100 Grows $52B as PERE 200 Stalls: Capital Concentrates at the Top
item.description: PERE 100 firms added $52B in fundraising over the past year while the PERE 200 stalled. Capital concentration accelerates as LPs favor scale and track record.

news:news.news:title: PERE 100 Grows $52B as PERE 200 Stalls: Capital Concentrates at the Top
news:news.news:keywords: PERE 100, PERE 200, private equity real estate, fundraising, capital concentration, LP behavior, institutional investors

item.title: Bond Traders Bet on Fed Hike as Jobs Data Looms Over CRE Debt Markets
item.description: Bond traders are betting the US economy will force a Fed rate hike by 2027. This week's jobs report will test that wager, with direct implications for CRE debt costs and cap rates.

news:news.news:title: Bond Traders Bet on Fed Hike as Jobs Data Looms Over CRE Debt Markets
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, interest rates, jobs report, bond market, CRE debt

item.title: Powell Warns Fed Credibility at Risk as Political Pressure Mounts
item.description: Jerome Powell warns the Fed's credibility is under 'stress test' in first remarks since leaving office. Political pressure and criminal probes threaten independence.

news:news.news:title: Powell Warns Fed Credibility at Risk as Political Pressure Mounts
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, Jerome Powell, central bank independence, monetary policy, Trump administration

item.title: S3 Capital Bets $132M on Student Housing in Supply-Starved Washington Heights
item.description: S3 Capital provides $131.5M construction loan to Edge Property Group for a 276-unit student housing tower at 465 West 165th Street in Washington Heights, a market with 0.8% vacancy.

news:news.news:title: S3 Capital Bets $132M on Student Housing in Supply-Starved Washington Heights
news:news.news:keywords: S3 Capital, Edge Property Group, student housing, Washington Heights, construction financing

item.title: Blackstone's $78B Debt Machine Turns to For-Sale Home Construction
item.description: Blackstone commits its $78B debt platform to finance 50,000+ for-sale homes annually, responding to political pressure and a 4-million-home supply gap.

news:news.news:title: Blackstone's $78B Debt Machine Turns to For-Sale Home Construction
news:news.news:keywords: Blackstone, homebuilding, construction lending, housing supply, institutional investors

item.title: Berkshire's $6.8B Taylor Morrison Bet Signals Abel's Deal Era
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway acquires Taylor Morrison for $6.8B in cash, a 24% premium. Greg Abel's first major deal signals a bet on housing recovery and a shift in Berkshire's capital deployment strategy.

news:news.news:title: Berkshire's $6.8B Taylor Morrison Bet Signals Abel's Deal Era
news:news.news:keywords: Berkshire Hathaway, Taylor Morrison, Greg Abel, homebuilding, M&A, housing market, Warren Buffett

item.title: SL Green Sells Midtown Tower for $312M as $2.5B Asset Sale Plan Accelerates
item.description: SL Green sells 10 East 53rd Street to Meadow Partners for $312M, or $800/sf, as part of a $2.5B asset sale plan. The mid-5% cap rate reflects improving Class A demand but persistent rate headwinds.

news:news.news:title: SL Green Sells Midtown Tower for $312M as $2.5B Asset Sale Plan Accelerates
news:news.news:keywords: SL Green, Meadow Partners, 10 East 53rd Street, office sales, Manhattan

item.title: Abdu Salman Files Permits for 21-Unit Bronx Condo Project at 2958 White Plains Road
item.description: Abdu Salman files permits for a 21-unit condo project at 2958 White Plains Road in the Bronx. The 18,579 SF building targets transit-oriented buyers near the 2/5 train.

news:news.news:title: Abdu Salman Files Permits for 21-Unit Bronx Condo Project at 2958 White Plains Road
news:news.news:keywords: Bronx, condo development, permits, Abdu Salman, transit-oriented development

item.title: CRE's AI Enthusiasm Meets a Training Void: 54% Offer No Instruction
item.description: MRI Software survey of 600 CRE pros finds 54% of organizations offer no AI training, despite 62% claiming readiness. Source validation and prompting skills are rare.

news:news.news:title: CRE's AI Enthusiasm Meets a Training Void: 54% Offer No Instruction
news:news.news:keywords: AI, CRE, training, MRI Software, productivity

item.title: HL&S LLC Files Permits for 45-Unit East Flatbush Project as Brooklyn Development Pipeline Tightens
item.description: HL&S LLC files permits for a 45-unit, 9-story mixed-use building at 885 New York Avenue in East Flatbush. The 33,142-sf project includes 30,071 sf of residential space and 3,070 sf of community facility space. Architect Yoel Rozenberg of JAY Architect & Engineering D.P.C. is listed.

news:news.news:title: HL&S LLC Files Permits for 45-Unit East Flatbush Project as Brooklyn Development Pipeline Tightens
news:news.news:keywords: HL&S LLC, East Flatbush, Brooklyn development, mixed-use, permits

item.title: AfDB Pledges $650M to Uganda Railway as China Belt and Road Falters
item.description: African Development Bank allocates $650M for Uganda's Kampala-Malaba railway, filling a gap as Chinese Belt and Road lending slows. A test of multilateral infrastructure finance.

news:news.news:title: AfDB Pledges $650M to Uganda Railway as China Belt and Road Falters
news:news.news:keywords: African Development Bank, Uganda, railway, infrastructure finance, Belt and Road

item.title: NYC Hotel Owners Sign $100K Housekeeper Deal as Labor Costs Surge
item.description: NYC hotel union contract raises housekeeper wages to $61/hr by 2031, adding $21.20/hr over eight years. Owners warn of closures as labor costs climb.

news:news.news:title: NYC Hotel Owners Sign $100K Housekeeper Deal as Labor Costs Surge
news:news.news:keywords: hotel union, wage increase, New York City, Hotel and Gaming Trades Council, labor costs

item.title: JPMorgan Reclads Bear Stearns Legacy at 383 Madison as Office Market Shifts
item.description: JPMorgan Chase reclads 383 Madison Avenue, the former Bear Stearns tower, in a $1B+ renovation. The 1.2M sf project signals institutional flight to quality as office vacancy persists.

news:news.news:title: JPMorgan Reclads Bear Stearns Legacy at 383 Madison as Office Market Shifts
news:news.news:keywords: JPMorgan Chase, 383 Madison Avenue, Bear Stearns, office renovation, flight to quality

item.title: Broadway Producer Sells UWS Mansion at 65% Discount as Market Reprices
item.description: Broadway producer William DeSeta sells 11,700 SF UWS mansion for $7M, down from $20M ask in 2017. The 65% discount signals a structural repricing of trophy residential assets.

news:news.news:title: Broadway Producer Sells UWS Mansion at 65% Discount as Market Reprices
news:news.news:keywords: Upper West Side, townhouse, repricing, William DeSeta, Kassin Sabbagh Realty

item.title: Michigan AG Challenges Data Center Subsidies as AI Infrastructure Boom Hits Local Resistance
item.description: Michigan AG Dana Nessel challenges data center approval near Ann Arbor, arguing ratepayers need transparency on contracts and costs. The case tests the subsidy model underpinning the AI infrastructure boom.

news:news.news:title: Michigan AG Challenges Data Center Subsidies as AI Infrastructure Boom Hits Local Resistance
news:news.news:keywords: data centers, AI infrastructure, Michigan AG, DigitalBridge, ratepayer transparency, subsidies

item.title: Mamdani Housing Plan: Winners, Losers, and the New York City Capital Stack
item.description: Mayor Mamdani's 'Block by Block' plan targets 200,000 affordable units, expands 7A program, and offers $75M co-op loans. Winners: Related, Essence, nonprofits. Losers: distressed rent-stabilized owners.

news:news.news:title: Mamdani Housing Plan: Winners, Losers, and the New York City Capital Stack
news:news.news:keywords: Mamdani, Block by Block, affordable housing, 7A program, COPA, SAFER Homes, REBNY, NYC housing policy, rent-stabilized

item.title: Paramount Skydance Stretches $110B Warner Debt to Breaking Point
item.description: Paramount Skydance Corp. pushes $110B Warner Bros. Discovery LBO debt to 7x EBITDA, testing lender appetite as syndication stalls and spreads widen.

news:news.news:title: Paramount Skydance Stretches $110B Warner Debt to Breaking Point
news:news.news:keywords: Paramount Skydance, Warner Bros. Discovery, LBO debt, syndication, leverage

item.title: Second Horizon Capital Pays $56.3M for Melbourne Square Mall
item.description: Second Horizon Capital acquires 710,000-sf Melbourne Square mall for $56.3M ($79/sf). Neuberger Berman and Cross Ocean Partners sell as capital improvement program begins.

news:news.news:title: Second Horizon Capital Pays $56.3M for Melbourne Square Mall
news:news.news:keywords: Second Horizon Capital, Melbourne Square, Florida retail, shopping center acquisition, Neuberger Berman

item.title: Two Harbors Vote Delayed Again as UWM and CCM Bidding War Enters Third Act
item.description: Two Harbors adjourns special meeting to June 11 after stockholders twice reject CrossCountry Mortgage's $12/share deal. UWM's $12.50 bid gains traction in a $1.3B battle for mortgage servicing scale.

news:news.news:title: Two Harbors Vote Delayed Again as UWM and CCM Bidding War Enters Third Act
news:news.news:keywords: Two Harbors, UWM, CrossCountry Mortgage, mortgage servicing rights, M&A bidding war

item.title: Benefit Street Partners Writes $82M Refi for Sarasota Multifamily as Lenders Return to Sun Belt
item.description: Benefit Street Partners provides $82.1M refi for Render Legacy Trail, a 450-unit Class A multifamily in Sarasota County. Three-year loan with extensions reflects lender return to Sun Belt.

news:news.news:title: Benefit Street Partners Writes $82M Refi for Sarasota Multifamily as Lenders Return to Sun Belt
news:news.news:keywords: Benefit Street Partners, Crescent Communities, FCP, multifamily refinance, Sarasota

item.title: Cabot Properties Acquires 1M SF Fort Worth Industrial Portfolio at Full Occupancy
item.description: Cabot Properties buys 1.03M SF class A industrial portfolio in Fort Worth, TX. Three buildings, 100% leased, 32-36' clear heights. Institutional strategy targets supply-constrained markets.

news:news.news:title: Cabot Properties Acquires 1M SF Fort Worth Industrial Portfolio at Full Occupancy
news:news.news:keywords: Cabot Properties, industrial real estate, Fort Worth, portfolio acquisition, institutional capital

item.title: Fed's Bowman Rejects Rate Hike Path as Inflation Surges to 3.8%
item.description: Fed Vice Chair Bowman rejects rate hikes to combat 3.8% PCE inflation, warning oil-driven price spikes are temporary and tightening would harm jobs and growth.

news:news.news:title: Fed's Bowman Rejects Rate Hike Path as Inflation Surges to 3.8%
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, Michelle Bowman, inflation, PCE, interest rates, monetary policy

item.title: 18 Indicted in Manhattan Deed Theft Scheme: A $1.6M Mortgage on a Stolen Brownstone
item.description: 18 indicted in Manhattan for deed theft and mortgage fraud on a West 131st St brownstone. DA Bragg details a $1.6M scheme using forged documents and straw buyers. Implications for title insurance and lender due diligence.

news:news.news:title: 18 Indicted in Manhattan Deed Theft Scheme: A $1.6M Mortgage on a Stolen Brownstone
news:news.news:keywords: deed theft, mortgage fraud, Manhattan DA, Alvin Bragg, title insurance

item.title: CBRE IM and Accelerate Close $630M Infrastructure Raise, Platform Hits $1.26B
item.description: CBRE IM and Accelerate Infrastructure close $630M capital raise, bringing total equity to $1.26B. Mubadala, Australian Retirement Trust join as investors.

news:news.news:title: CBRE IM and Accelerate Close $630M Infrastructure Raise, Platform Hits $1.26B
news:news.news:keywords: CBRE Investment Management, Accelerate Infrastructure, Mubadala, Australian Retirement Trust, digital infrastructure, capital raise, institutional investors

item.title: Rockrose Locks $404M Freddie Mac Refi on West Village Landmark
item.description: Rockrose Development secures $404M Freddie Mac loan via PNC Bank to refinance 666 Greenwich Street. The 472-unit landmarked Archive building deal signals agency appetite for stabilized multifamily.

news:news.news:title: Rockrose Locks $404M Freddie Mac Refi on West Village Landmark
news:news.news:keywords: Rockrose Development, Freddie Mac, PNC Bank, 666 Greenwich Street, multifamily refinance

item.title: CoStar Pays $800M Cash for Zonda: Data Monopoly Meets Homebuilding
item.description: CoStar Group acquires Zonda for $800M cash from MidOcean Partners. The deal adds 3,000 construction clients and a lot-level database to CoStar's residential expansion, following Homesnap, Homes.com, and Matterport.

news:news.news:title: CoStar Pays $800M Cash for Zonda: Data Monopoly Meets Homebuilding
news:news.news:keywords: CoStar Group, Zonda, MidOcean Partners, M&A, real estate data, homebuilding, construction technology

item.title: Fed's Schmid Signals Rate Pause as Inflation Stays Sticky Above 3%
item.description: Kansas City Fed President Jeff Schmid says officials must signal commitment to inflation fight. Markets now price no rate cut before September 2026 as core PCE holds above 3%.

news:news.news:title: Fed's Schmid Signals Rate Pause as Inflation Stays Sticky Above 3%
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve, Jeff Schmid, inflation, monetary policy, interest rates

item.title: DLC and DRA Advisors Pay $95M for Shadow Lake Towne Center: A Bet on Grocery-Anchored Retail
item.description: DLC Management and DRA Advisors acquire Shadow Lake Towne Center in Papillion, Nebraska, for $95 million. The 640,327-sf center is 89% occupied with national tenants; Hy-Vee grocery not included.

news:news.news:title: DLC and DRA Advisors Pay $95M for Shadow Lake Towne Center: A Bet on Grocery-Anchored Retail
news:news.news:keywords: DLC Management, DRA Advisors, Shadow Lake Towne Center, Omaha retail, grocery-anchored

item.title: SL Green and Mori Building Bet $175M on Midtown Trophy Office as Flight to Quality Intensifies
item.description: SL Green and Mori Building will develop 850K SF office tower at 346 Madison Ave. SL Green sold 49% stake at $175M valuation. One Vanderbilt valued at $4.7B. Manhattan leasing strongest since 2019.

news:news.news:title: SL Green and Mori Building Bet $175M on Midtown Trophy Office as Flight to Quality Intensifies
news:news.news:keywords: SL Green, Mori Building, trophy office, Midtown Manhattan, flight to quality

item.title: Hines, MetLife JV Refinances West Virginia Industrial Park at $60/SF
item.description: Hines and MetLife JV secure $43.8M from Mesa West Capital to refinance two Class A industrial buildings in Martinsburg, WV. One building vacant; loan signals lender appetite for modern logistics near I-81.

news:news.news:title: Hines, MetLife JV Refinances West Virginia Industrial Park at $60/SF
news:news.news:keywords: Hines, MetLife Investment Management, Mesa West Capital, industrial refinance, Tabler Station Logistics Park

item.title: Google's $15B Missouri Bet: Hyperscaler Infrastructure Defies CRE Slowdown
item.description: Google invests $15B in New Florence, MO data center, creating thousands of jobs and 1+ GW of new capacity. The deal underscores hyperscaler capital allocation decoupled from traditional CRE headwinds.

news:news.news:title: Google's $15B Missouri Bet: Hyperscaler Infrastructure Defies CRE Slowdown
news:news.news:keywords: Google, data center, Missouri, infrastructure, hyperscaler

item.title: Walker & Dunlop's $101.6M HUD Loan: The New Normal for Multifamily Refinance
item.description: Walker & Dunlop arranges $101.6M HUD 223(f) loan for Enclave Heritage Flats in Chula Vista, refinancing 2024 debt. Agency lending fills the gap as CMBS and bank liquidity retreat.

news:news.news:title: Walker & Dunlop's $101.6M HUD Loan: The New Normal for Multifamily Refinance
news:news.news:keywords: Walker & Dunlop, HUD 223(f), multifamily, Chula Vista, agency lending, refinance, Baldwin Asset Management

item.title: Rotem Rosen Secures $135M Israeli Bond Refinancing as Market Reopens
item.description: Rotem Rosen's MRR Development secures $135M in Israeli bond financing at 6.2% for Hotel Indigo NYC and Miami development site, signaling renewed investor appetite after defaults.

news:news.news:title: Rotem Rosen Secures $135M Israeli Bond Refinancing as Market Reopens
news:news.news:keywords: Israeli bond market, Rotem Rosen, MRR Development, Hotel Indigo, Miami development

item.title: Harbor Group, Partners Refinance $351M Multifamily Portfolio with ACRE
item.description: Harbor Group International, The Garrett Cos., and Telis Group refinance $351M in senior construction debt across eight multifamily properties totaling 1,573 units. ACRE originated the facility.

news:news.news:title: Harbor Group, Partners Refinance $351M Multifamily Portfolio with ACRE
news:news.news:keywords: Harbor Group International, ACRE, multifamily refinance, construction debt, The Garrett Cos.

item.title: Kayne Anderson's $5B Close Signals Institutional Flight to Scale in Real Estate
item.description: Kayne Anderson closes $5B opportunistic fund, the largest private real estate vehicle this year. Stoneshield Capital also sets a Southern Europe record. Institutional capital is consolidating among elite managers.

news:news.news:title: Kayne Anderson's $5B Close Signals Institutional Flight to Scale in Real Estate
news:news.news:keywords: Kayne Anderson, fundraising, opportunistic real estate, Stoneshield Capital, institutional capital, private equity real estate

item.title: Tanger Pays $60M for Ohio Town Center as Open-Air Retail Consolidation Accelerates
item.description: Tanger acquires The Town Center at Levis Commons in Perrysburg, OH for $60M ($200/SF). Hill Partners sells 300K SF mixed-use asset as open-air retail consolidation intensifies.

news:news.news:title: Tanger Pays $60M for Ohio Town Center as Open-Air Retail Consolidation Accelerates
news:news.news:keywords: Tanger, Hill Partners, Levis Commons, open-air retail, REIT acquisition, Perrysburg Ohio

item.title: ACRE Lends $351M on Harbor Group's Suburban Multifamily Refi
item.description: ACRE provides $351M loan to Harbor Group International, Garrett Companies, and Telis Group to refinance eight suburban multifamily properties in four states, replacing construction debt.

news:news.news:title: ACRE Lends $351M on Harbor Group's Suburban Multifamily Refi
news:news.news:keywords: ACRE, Harbor Group International, multifamily refinance, suburban multifamily, construction debt
